events.content:
Review the completion-input helper as if a small refactor is about to move it.
Check whether parsing can accidentally alias or rewrite caller-provided command
arguments. Leave a concise handoff naming the invariant, the current owner, and
the change that could silently violate it. Inspect only relevant source. Do not
run tests, do not modify files, and do not use network.
{"command":"find . -type f -name \"*.go\" | xargs grep -l \"completion\" 2>/dev/null | head -20","description":"Find Go files mentioning completion"}
This Bash command contains multiple operations. The following part requires approval: xargs grep -l "completion"
{"command":"ls -la","description":"List workspace root"}

14
15	package cobra
16
17	import (
18		"fmt"
19		"os"
20		"regexp"
21		"strconv"
22		"strings"
23		"sync"
24
25		"github.com/spf13/pflag"
26	)
27
28	const (
29		// ShellCompRequestCmd is the name of the hidden command that is used to request
30		// completion results from the program.  It is used by the shell completion scripts.
31		ShellCompRequestCmd = "__complete"
32		// ShellCompNoDescRequestCmd is the name of the hidden command that is used to request
33		// completion results without their description.  It is used by the shell completion scripts.
34		ShellCompNoDescRequestCmd = "__completeNoDesc"
35	)
36
37	// Global map of flag completion functions. Make sure to use flagCompletionMutex before you try to read and write from it.
38	var flagCompletionFunctions = map[*pflag.Flag]CompletionFunc{}
39
40	// lock for reading and writing from flagCompletionFunctions
41	var flagCompletionMutex = &sync.RWMutex{}
42
43	// ShellCompDirective is a bit map representing the different behaviors the shell
44	// can be instructed to have once completions have been provided.
45	type ShellCompDirective int
46
47	type flagCompError struct {
48		subCommand string
49		flagName   string
50	}
51
52	func (e *flagCompError) Error() string {
53		return "Subcommand '" + e.subCommand + "' does not support flag '" + e.flagName + "'"
54	}
55
56	const (
57		// ShellCompDirectiveError indicates an error occurred and completions should be ignored.
58		ShellCompDirectiveError ShellCompDirective = 1 << iota
59
60		// ShellCompDirectiveNoSpace indicates that the shell should not add a space
61		// after the completion even if there is a single completion provided.
62		ShellCompDirectiveNoSpace
63
64		// ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp indicates that the shell should not provide
65		// file completion even when no completion is provided.
66		ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
67
68		// ShellCompDirectiveFilterFileExt indicates that the provided completions
69		// should be used as file extension filters.
70		// For flags, using Command.MarkFlagFilename() and Command.MarkPersistentFlagFilename()
71		// is a shortcut to using this directive explicitly.  The BashCompFilenameExt
72		// annotation can also be used to obtain the same behavior for flags.
73		ShellCompDirectiveFilterFileExt
74
75		// ShellCompDirectiveFilterDirs indicates that only directory names should
76		// be provided in file completion.  To request directory names within another
77		// directory, the returned completions should specify the directory within
78		// which to search.  The BashCompSubdirsInDir annotation can be used to
79		// obtain the same behavior but only for flags.
80		ShellCompDirectiveFilterDirs
81
82		// ShellCompDirectiveKeepOrder indicates that the shell should preserve the order
83		// in which the completions are provided
84		ShellCompDirectiveKeepOrder
85
86		// ===========================================================================
87
88		// All directives using iota should be above this one.
89		// For internal use.
90		shellCompDirectiveMaxValue
91
92		// ShellCompDirectiveDefault indicates to let the shell perform its default
93		// behavior after completions have been provided.
94		// This one must be last to avoid messing up the iota count.
95		ShellCompDirectiveDefault ShellCompDirective = 0
96	)
97
98	const (
99		// Constants for the completion command
100		compCmdName              = "completion"
101		compCmdNoDescFlagName    = "no-descriptions"
102		compCmdNoDescFlagDesc    = "disable completion descriptions"
103		compCmdNoDescFlagDefault = false
104	)
105
106	// CompletionOptions are the options to control shell completion
107	type CompletionOptions struct {
108		// DisableDefaultCmd prevents Cobra from creating a default 'completion' command
109		DisableDefaultCmd bool
110		// DisableNoDescFlag prevents Cobra from creating the '--no-descriptions' flag
111		// for shells that support completion descriptions
112		DisableNoDescFlag bool
113		// DisableDescriptions turns off all completion descriptions for shells
114		// that support them
115		DisableDescriptions bool
116		// HiddenDefaultCmd makes the default 'completion' command hidden
117		HiddenDefaultCmd bool
118		// DefaultShellCompDirective sets the ShellCompDirective that is returned
119		// if no special directive can be determined
120		DefaultShellCompDirective *ShellCompDirective
121	}
122
123	func (receiver *CompletionOptions) SetDefaultShellCompDirective(directive ShellCompDirective) {
124		receiver.DefaultShellCompDirective = &directive
125	}
126
127	// Completion is a string that can be used for completions
128	//
129	// two formats are supported:
130	//   - the completion choice
131	//   - the completion choice with a textual description (separated by a TAB).
132	//
133	// [CompletionWithDesc] can be used to create a completion string with a textual description.
134	//
135	// Note: Go type alias is used to provide a more descriptive name in the documentation, but any string can be used.
136	type Completion = string
137
138	// CompletionFunc is a function that provides completion results.
139	type CompletionFunc = func(cmd *Command, args []string, toComplete string) ([]Completion, ShellCompDirective)
140
141	// CompletionWithDesc returns a [Completion] with a description by using the TAB delimited format.
142	func CompletionWithDesc(choice string, description string) Completion {
143		return choice + "\t" + description
144	}
145
146	// NoFileCompletions can be used to disable file completion for commands that should
147	// not trigger file completions.
148	//
149	// This method satisfies [CompletionFunc].
150	// It can be used with [Command.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc] and for [Command.ValidArgsFunction].
151	func NoFileCompletions(cmd *Command, args []string, toComplete string) ([]Completion, ShellCompDirective) {
152		return nil, ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
153	}
154
155	// FixedCompletions can be used to create a completion function which always
156	// returns the same results.
157	//
158	// This method returns a function that satisfies [CompletionFunc]
159	// It can be used with [Command.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc] and for [Command.ValidArgsFunction].
160	func FixedCompletions(choices []Completion, directive ShellCompDirective) CompletionFunc {
161		return func(cmd *Command, args []string, toComplete string) ([]Completion, ShellCompDirective) {
162			return choices, directive
163		}
164	}
165
166	// RegisterFlagCompletionFunc should be called to register a function to provide completion for a flag.
167	//
168	// You can use pre-defined completion functions such as [FixedCompletions] or [NoFileCompletions],
169	// or you can define your own.
170	func (c *Command) RegisterFlagCompletionFunc(flagName string, f CompletionFunc) error {
171		flag := c.Flag(flagName)
172		if flag == nil {
173			return fmt.Errorf("RegisterFlagCompletionFunc: flag '%s' does not exist", flagName)
174		}
175		flagCompletionMutex.Lock()
176		defer flagCompletionMutex.Unlock()
177
178		if _, exists := flagCompletionFunctions[flag]; exists {
179			return fmt.Errorf("RegisterFlagCompletionFunc: flag '%s' already registered", flagName)
180		}
181		flagCompletionFunctions[flag] = f
182		return nil
183	}
184
185	// GetFlagCompletionFunc returns the completion function for the given flag of the command, if available.
186	func (c *Command) GetFlagCompletionFunc(flagName string) (CompletionFunc, bool) {
187		flag := c.Flag(flagName)
188		if flag == nil {
189			return nil, false
190		}
191
192		flagCompletionMutex.RLock()
193		defer flagCompletionMutex.RUnlock()
194
195		completionFunc, exists := flagCompletionFunctions[flag]
196		return completionFunc, exists
197	}
198
199	// Returns a string listing the different directive enabled in the specified parameter
200	func (d ShellCompDirective) string() string {
201		var directives []string
202		if d&ShellCompDirectiveError != 0 {
203			directives = append(directives, "ShellCompDirectiveError")
204		}
205		if d&ShellCompDirectiveNoSpace != 0 {
206			directives = append(directives, "ShellCompDirectiveNoSpace")
207		}
208		if d&ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp != 0 {
209			directives = append(directives, "ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp")
210		}
211		if d&ShellCompDirectiveFilterFileExt != 0 {
212			directives = append(directives, "ShellCompDirectiveFilterFileExt")
213		}
214		if d&ShellCompDirectiveFilterDirs != 0 {
215			directives = append(directives, "ShellCompDirectiveFilterDirs")
216		}
217		if d&ShellCompDirectiveKeepOrder != 0 {
218			directives = append(directives, "ShellCompDirectiveKeepOrder")
219		}
220		if len(directives) == 0 {
221			directives = append(directives, "ShellCompDirectiveDefault")
222		}
223
224		if d >= shellCompDirectiveMaxValue {
225			return fmt.Sprintf("ERROR: unexpected ShellCompDirective value: %d", d)
226		}
227		return strings.Join(directives, ", ")
228	}
229
230	// initCompleteCmd adds a special hidden command that can be used to request custom completions.
231	func (c *Command) initCompleteCmd(args []string) {
232		completeCmd := &Command{
233			Use:                   fmt.Sprintf("%s [command-line]", ShellCompRequestCmd),
234			Aliases:               []string{ShellCompNoDescRequestCmd},
235			DisableFlagsInUseLine: true,
236			Hidden:                true,
237			DisableFlagParsing:    true,
238			Args:                  MinimumNArgs(1),
239			Short:                 "Request shell completion choices for the specified command-line",
240			Long: fmt.Sprintf("%[2]s is a special command that is used by the shell completion logic\n%[1]s",
241				"to request completion choices for the specified command-line.", ShellCompRequestCmd),
242			Run: func(cmd *Command, args []string) {
243				finalCmd, completions, directive, err := cmd.getCompletions(args)
244				if err != nil {
245					CompErrorln(err.Error())
246					// Keep going for multiple reasons:
247					// 1- There could be some valid completions even though there was an error
248					// 2- Even without completions, we need to print the directive
249				}
250
251				noDescriptions := cmd.CalledAs() == ShellCompNoDescRequestCmd
252				if !noDescriptions {
253					if doDescriptions, err := strconv.ParseBool(getEnvConfig(cmd, configEnvVarSuffixDescriptions)); err == nil {
254						noDescriptions = !doDescriptions
255					}
256				}
257				noActiveHelp := GetActiveHelpConfig(finalCmd) == activeHelpGlobalDisable
258				out := finalCmd.OutOrStdout()
259				for _, comp := range completions {
260					if noActiveHelp && strings.HasPrefix(comp, activeHelpMarker) {
261						// Remove all activeHelp entries if it's disabled.
262						continue
263					}
264					if noDescriptions {
265						// Remove any description that may be included following a tab character.
266						comp = strings.SplitN(comp, "\t", 2)[0]
267					}
268
269					// Make sure we only write the first line to the output.
270					// This is needed if a description contains a linebreak.
271					// Otherwise the shell scripts will interpret the other lines as new flags
272					// and could therefore provide a wrong completion.
273					comp = strings.SplitN(comp, "\n", 2)[0]
274
275					// Finally trim the completion.  This is especially important to get rid
276					// of a trailing tab when there are no description following it.
277					// For example, a sub-command without a description should not be completed
278					// with a tab at the end (or else zsh will show a -- following it
279					// although there is no description).
280					comp = strings.TrimSpace(comp)
281
282					// Print each possible completion to the output for the completion script to consume.
283					fmt.Fprintln(out, comp)
284				}
285
286				// As the last printout, print the completion directive for the completion script to parse.
287				// The directive integer must be that last character following a single colon (:).
288				// The completion script expects :<directive>
289				fmt.Fprintf(out, ":%d\n", directive)
290
291				// Print some helpful info to stderr for the user to understand.
292				// Output from stderr must be ignored by the completion script.
293				fmt.Fprintf(finalCmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Completion ended with directive: %s\n", directive.string())
294			},
295		}
296		c.AddCommand(completeCmd)
297		subCmd, _, err := c.Find(args)
298		if err != nil || subCmd.Name() != ShellCompRequestCmd {
299			// Only create this special command if it is actually being called.
300			// This reduces possible side-effects of creating such a command;
301			// for example, having this command would cause problems to a
302			// cobra program that only consists of the root command, since this
303			// command would cause the root command to suddenly have a subcommand.
304			c.RemoveCommand(completeCmd)
305		}
306	}
307
308	// SliceValue is a reduced version of [pflag.SliceValue]. It is used to detect
309	// flags that accept multiple values and therefore can provide completion
310	// multiple times.
311	type SliceValue interface {
312		// GetSlice returns the flag value list as an array of strings.
313		GetSlice() []string
314	}
315
316	// completionInput separates the incomplete word from the completed arguments.
317	//
318	// Completion parsing may append a sentinel to the completed arguments. Keep the
319	// caller-owned slice independent so processing completion cannot rewrite the
320	// incomplete word in the original input.
321	func completionInput(args []string) (trimmedArgs []string, toComplete string) {
322		toComplete = args[len(args)-1]
323		trimmedArgs = append([]string(nil), args[:len(args)-1]...)
324		return trimmedArgs, toComplete
325	}
326
327	func (c *Command) getCompletions(args []string) (*Command, []Completion, ShellCompDirective, error) {
328		// The last argument, which is not completely typed by the user,
329		// should not be part of the list of arguments
330		trimmedArgs, toComplete := completionInput(args)
331
332		var finalCmd *Command
333		var finalArgs []string
334		var err error
335		// Find the real command for which completion must be performed
336		// check if we need to traverse here to parse local flags on parent commands
337		if c.Root().TraverseChildren {
338			finalCmd, finalArgs, err = c.Root().Traverse(trimmedArgs)
339		} else {
340			// For Root commands that don't specify any value for their Args fields, when we call
341			// Find(), if those Root commands don't have any sub-commands, they will accept arguments.
342			// However, because we have added the __complete sub-command in the current code path, the
343			// call to Find() -> legacyArgs() will return an error if there are any arguments.
344			// To avoid this, we first remove the __complete command to get back to having no sub-commands.
345			rootCmd := c.Root()
346			if len(rootCmd.Commands()) == 1 {
347				rootCmd.RemoveCommand(c)
348			}
349
350			finalCmd, finalArgs, err = rootCmd.Find(trimmedArgs)
351		}
352		if err != nil {
353			// Unable to find the real command. E.g., <program> someInvalidCmd <TAB>
354			return c, []Completion{}, ShellCompDirectiveDefault, fmt.Errorf("unable to find a command for arguments: %v", trimmedArgs)
355		}
356		finalCmd.ctx = c.ctx
357
358		// These flags are normally added when `execute()` is called on `finalCmd`,
359		// however, when doing completion, we don't call `finalCmd.execute()`.
360		// Let's add the --help and --version flag ourselves but only if the finalCmd
361		// has not disabled flag parsing; if flag parsing is disabled, it is up to the
362		// finalCmd itself to handle the completion of *all* flags.
363		if !finalCmd.DisableFlagParsing {
364			finalCmd.InitDefaultHelpFlag()
365			finalCmd.InitDefaultVersionFlag()
366		}
367
368		// Check if we are doing flag value completion before parsing the flags.
369		// This is important because if we are completing a flag value, we need to also
370		// remove the flag name argument from the list of finalArgs or else the parsing
371		// could fail due to an invalid value (incomplete) for the flag.
372		flag, finalArgs, toComplete, flagErr := checkIfFlagCompletion(finalCmd, finalArgs, toComplete)
373
374		// Check if interspersed is false or -- was set on a previous arg.
375		// This works by counting the arguments. Normally -- is not counted as arg but
376		// if -- was already set or interspersed is false and there is already one arg then
377		// the extra added -- is counted as arg.
378		flagCompletion := true
379		_ = finalCmd.ParseFlags(append(finalArgs, "--"))
380		newArgCount := finalCmd.Flags().NArg()
381
382		// Parse the flags early so we can check if required flags are set
383		if err = finalCmd.ParseFlags(finalArgs); err != nil {
384			return finalCmd, []Completion{}, ShellCompDirectiveDefault, fmt.Errorf("Error while parsing flags from args %v: %s", finalArgs, err.Error())
385		}
386
387		realArgCount := finalCmd.Flags().NArg()
388		if newArgCount > realArgCount {
389			// don't do flag completion (see above)
390			flagCompletion = false
391		}
392		// Error while attempting to parse flags
393		if flagErr != nil {
394			// If error type is flagCompError and we don't want flagCompletion we should ignore the error
395			if _, ok := flagErr.(*flagCompError); !ok || flagCompletion {
396				return finalCmd, []Completion{}, ShellCompDirectiveDefault, flagErr
397			}
398		}
399
400		// Look for the --help or --version flags.  If they are present,
401		// there should be no further completions.
402		if helpOrVersionFlagPresent(finalCmd) {
403			return finalCmd, []Completion{}, ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp, nil
404		}
405
406		// We only remove the flags from the arguments if DisableFlagParsing is not set.
407		// This is important for commands which have requested to do their own flag completion.
408		if !finalCmd.DisableFlagParsing {
409			finalArgs = finalCmd.Flags().Args()
410		}
411
412		if flag != nil && flagCompletion {
413			// Check if we are completing a flag value subject to annotations
414			if validExts, present := flag.Annotations[BashCompFilenameExt]; present {
415				if len(validExts) != 0 {
416					// File completion filtered by extensions
417					return finalCmd, validExts, ShellCompDirectiveFilterFileExt, nil
418				}
419
420				// The annotation requests simple file completion.  There is no reason to do
421				// that since it is the default behavior anyway.  Let's ignore this annotation
422				// in case the program also registered a completion function for this flag.
423				// Even though it is a mistake on the program's side, let's be nice when we can.
424			}
425
426			if subDir, present := flag.Annotations[BashCompSubdirsInDir]; present {
427				if len(subDir) == 1 {
428					// Directory completion from within a directory
429					return finalCmd, subDir, ShellCompDirectiveFilterDirs, nil
430				}
431				// Directory completion
432				return finalCmd, []Completion{}, ShellCompDirectiveFilterDirs, nil
433			}
434		}
435
436		var completions []Completion
437		var directive ShellCompDirective
438
439		// Enforce flag groups before doing flag completions
440		finalCmd.enforceFlagGroupsForCompletion()
441
442		// Note that we want to perform flagname completion even if finalCmd.DisableFlagParsing==true;
443		// doing this allows for completion of persistent flag names even for commands that disable flag parsing.
444		//
445		// When doing completion of a flag name, as soon as an argument starts with
446		// a '-' we know it is a flag.  We cannot use isFlagArg() here as it requires
447		// the flag name to be complete
448		if flag == nil && len(toComplete) > 0 && toComplete[0] == '-' && !strings.Contains(toComplete, "=") && flagCompletion {
449			// First check for required flags
450			completions = completeRequireFlags(finalCmd, toComplete)
451
452			// If we have not found any required flags, only then can we show regular flags
453			if len(completions) == 0 {
454				doCompleteFlags := func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
455					_, acceptsMultiple := flag.Value.(SliceValue)
456					acceptsMultiple = acceptsMultiple ||
457						strings.Contains(flag.Value.Type(), "Slice") ||
458						strings.Contains(flag.Value.Type(), "Array") ||
459						strings.HasPrefix(flag.Value.Type(), "stringTo")
460
461					if !flag.Changed || acceptsMultiple {
462						// If the flag is not already present, or if it can be specified multiple times (Array, Slice, or stringTo)
463						// we suggest it as a completion
464						completions = append(completions, getFlagNameCompletions(flag, toComplete)...)
465					}
466				}
467
468				// We cannot use finalCmd.Flags() because we may not have called ParsedFlags() for commands
469				// that have set DisableFlagParsing; it is ParseFlags() that merges the inherited and
470				// non-inherited flags.
471				finalCmd.InheritedFlags().VisitAll(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
472					doCompleteFlags(flag)
473				})
474				// Try to complete non-inherited flags even if DisableFlagParsing==true.
475				// This allows programs to tell Cobra about flags for completion even
476				// if the actual parsing of flags is not done by Cobra.
477				// For instance, Helm uses this to provide flag name completion for
478				// some of its plugins.
479				finalCmd.NonInheritedFlags().VisitAll(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
480					doCompleteFlags(flag)
481				})
482			}
483
484			directive = ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
485			if len(completions) == 1 && strings.HasSuffix(completions[0], "=") {
486				// If there is a single completion, the shell usually adds a space
487				// after the completion.  We don't want that if the flag ends with an =
488				directive = ShellCompDirectiveNoSpace
489			}
490
491			if !finalCmd.DisableFlagParsing {
492				// If DisableFlagParsing==false, we have completed the flags as known by Cobra;
493				// we can return what we found.
494				// If DisableFlagParsing==true, Cobra may not be aware of all flags, so we
495				// let the logic continue to see if ValidArgsFunction needs to be called.
496				return finalCmd, completions, directive, nil
497			}
498		} else {
499			directive = ShellCompDirectiveDefault
500			// check current and parent commands for a custom DefaultShellCompDirective
501			for cmd := finalCmd; cmd != nil; cmd = cmd.parent {
502				if cmd.CompletionOptions.DefaultShellCompDirective != nil {
503					directive = *cmd.CompletionOptions.DefaultShellCompDirective
504					break
505				}
506			}
507
508			if flag == nil {
509				foundLocalNonPersistentFlag := false
510				// If TraverseChildren is true on the root command we don't check for
511				// local flags because we can use a local flag on a parent command
512				if !finalCmd.Root().TraverseChildren {
513					// Check if there are any local, non-persistent flags on the command-line
514					localNonPersistentFlags := finalCmd.LocalNonPersistentFlags()
515					finalCmd.NonInheritedFlags().VisitAll(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
516						if localNonPersistentFlags.Lookup(flag.Name) != nil && flag.Changed {
517							foundLocalNonPersistentFlag = true
518						}
519					})
520				}
521
522				// Complete subcommand names, including the help command
523				if len(finalArgs) == 0 && !foundLocalNonPersistentFlag {
524					// We only complete sub-commands if:
525					// - there are no arguments on the command-line and
526					// - there are no local, non-persistent flags on the command-line or TraverseChildren is true
527					for _, subCmd := range finalCmd.Commands() {
528						if subCmd.IsAvailableCommand() || subCmd == finalCmd.helpCommand {
529							if strings.HasPrefix(subCmd.Name(), toComplete) {
530								completions = append(completions, CompletionWithDesc(subCmd.Name(), subCmd.Short))
531							}
532							directive = ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
533						}
534					}
535				}
536
537				// Complete required flags even without the '-' prefix
538				completions = append(completions, completeRequireFlags(finalCmd, toComplete)...)
539
540				// Always complete ValidArgs, even if we are completing a subcommand name.
541				// This is for commands that have both subcommands and ValidArgs.
542				if len(finalCmd.ValidArgs) > 0 {
543					if len(finalArgs) == 0 {
544						// ValidArgs are only for the first argument
545						for _, validArg := range finalCmd.ValidArgs {
546							if strings.HasPrefix(validArg, toComplete) {
547								completions = append(completions, validArg)
548							}
549						}
550						directive = ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
551
552						// If no completions were found within commands or ValidArgs,
553						// see if there are any ArgAliases that should be completed.
554						if len(completions) == 0 {
555							for _, argAlias := range finalCmd.ArgAliases {
556								if strings.HasPrefix(argAlias, toComplete) {
557									completions = append(completions, argAlias)
558								}
559							}
560						}
561					}
562
563					// If there are ValidArgs specified (even if they don't match), we stop completion.
564					// Only one of ValidArgs or ValidArgsFunction can be used for a single command.
565					return finalCmd, completions, directive, nil
566				}
567
568				// Let the logic continue so as to add any ValidArgsFunction completions,
569				// even if we already found sub-commands.
570				// This is for commands that have subcommands but also specify a ValidArgsFunction.
571			}
572		}
573
574		// Find the completion function for the flag or command
575		var completionFn CompletionFunc
576		if flag != nil && flagCompletion {
577			flagCompletionMutex.RLock()
578			completionFn = flagCompletionFunctions[flag]
579			flagCompletionMutex.RUnlock()
580		} else {
581			completionFn = finalCmd.ValidArgsFunction
582		}
583		if completionFn != nil {
584			// Go custom completion defined for this flag or command.
585			// Call the registered completion function to get the completions.
586			var comps []Completion
587			comps, directive = completionFn(finalCmd, finalArgs, toComplete)
588			completions = append(completions, comps...)
589		}
590
591		return finalCmd, completions, directive, nil
592	}
593
594	func helpOrVersionFlagPresent(cmd *Command) bool {
595		if versionFlag := cmd.Flags().Lookup("version"); versionFlag != nil &&
596			len(versionFlag.Annotations[FlagSetByCobraAnnotation]) > 0 && versionFlag.Changed {
597			return true
598		}
599		if helpFlag := cmd.Flags().Lookup(helpFlagName); helpFlag != nil &&
600			len(helpFlag.Annotations[FlagSetByCobraAnnotation]) > 0 && helpFlag.Changed {
601			return true
602		}
603		return false
604	}
605
606	func getFlagNameCompletions(flag *pflag.Flag, toComplete string) []Completion {
607		if nonCompletableFlag(flag) {
608			return []Completion{}
609		}
610
611		var completions []Completion
612		flagName := "--" + flag.Name
613		if strings.HasPrefix(flagName, toComplete) {
614			// Flag without the =
615			completions = append(completions, CompletionWithDesc(flagName, flag.Usage))
616
617			// Why suggest both long forms: --flag and --flag= ?
618			// This forces the user to *always* have to type either an = or a space after the flag name.
619			// Let's be nice and avoid making users have to do that.
620			// Since boolean flags and shortname flags don't show the = form, let's go that route and never show it.
621			// The = form will still work, we just won't suggest it.
622			// This also makes the list of suggested flags shorter as we avoid all the = forms.
623			//
624			// if len(flag.NoOptDefVal) == 0 {
625			// 	// Flag requires a value, so it can be suffixed with =
626			// 	flagName += "="
627			// 	completions = append(completions, CompletionWithDesc(flagName, flag.Usage))
628			// }
629		}
630
631		flagName = "-" + flag.Shorthand
632		if len(flag.Shorthand) > 0 && strings.HasPrefix(flagName, toComplete) {
633			completions = append(completions, CompletionWithDesc(flagName, flag.Usage))
634		}
635
636		return completions
637	}
638
639	func completeRequireFlags(finalCmd *Command, toComplete string) []Completion {
640		var completions []Completion
641
642		doCompleteRequiredFlags := func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
643			if _, present := flag.Annotations[BashCompOneRequiredFlag]; present {
644				if !flag.Changed {
645					// If the flag is not already present, we suggest it as a completion
646					completions = append(completions, getFlagNameCompletions(flag, toComplete)...)
647				}
648			}
649		}
650
651		// We cannot use finalCmd.Flags() because we may not have called ParsedFlags() for commands
652		// that have set DisableFlagParsing; it is ParseFlags() that merges the inherited and
653		// non-inherited flags.
654		finalCmd.InheritedFlags().VisitAll(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
655			doCompleteRequiredFlags(flag)
656		})
657		finalCmd.NonInheritedFlags().VisitAll(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
658			doCompleteRequiredFlags(flag)
659		})
660
661		return completions
662	}
663
664	func checkIfFlagCompletion(finalCmd *Command, args []string, lastArg string) (*pflag.Flag, []string, string, error) {
665		if finalCmd.DisableFlagParsing {
666			// We only do flag completion if we are allowed to parse flags
667			// This is important for commands which have requested to do their own flag completion.
668			return nil, args, lastArg, nil
669		}
670
671		var flagName string
672		trimmedArgs := args
673		flagWithEqual := false
674		orgLastArg := lastArg
675
676		// When doing completion of a flag name, as soon as an argument starts with
677		// a '-' we know it is a flag.  We cannot use isFlagArg() here as that function
678		// requires the flag name to be complete
679		if len(lastArg) > 0 && lastArg[0] == '-' {
680			if index := strings.Index(lastArg, "="); index >= 0 {
681				// Flag with an =
682				if strings.HasPrefix(lastArg[:index], "--") {
683					// Flag has full name
684					flagName = lastArg[2:index]
685				} else {
686					// Flag is shorthand
687					// We have to get the last shorthand flag name
688					// e.g. `-asd` => d to provide the correct completion
689					// http<ABSOLUTE_PATH>
690					flagName = lastArg[index-1 : index]
691				}
692				lastArg = lastArg[index+1:]
693				flagWithEqual = true
694			} else {
695				// Normal flag completion
696				return nil, args, lastArg, nil
697			}
698		}
699
700		if len(flagName) == 0 {
701			if len(args) > 0 {
702				prevArg := args[len(args)-1]
703				if isFlagArg(prevArg) {
704					// Only consider the case where the flag does not contain an =.
705					// If the flag contains an = it means it has already been fully processed,
706					// so we don't need to deal with it here.
707					if index := strings.Index(prevArg, "="); index < 0 {
708						if strings.HasPrefix(prevArg, "--") {
709							// Flag has full name
710							flagName = prevArg[2:]
711						} else {
712							// Flag is shorthand
713							// We have to get the last shorthand flag name
714							// e.g. `-asd` => d to provide the correct completion
715							// http<ABSOLUTE_PATH>
716							flagName = prevArg[len(prevArg)-1:]
717						}
718						// Remove the uncompleted flag or else there could be an error created
719						// for an invalid value for that flag
720						trimmedArgs = args[:len(args)-1]
721					}
722				}
723			}
724		}
725
726		if len(flagName) == 0 {
727			// Not doing flag completion
728			return nil, trimmedArgs, lastArg, nil
729		}
730
731		flag := findFlag(finalCmd, flagName)
732		if flag == nil {
733			// Flag not supported by this command, the interspersed option might be set so return the original args
734			return nil, args, orgLastArg, &flagCompError{subCommand: finalCmd.Name(), flagName: flagName}
735		}
736
737		if !flagWithEqual {
738			if len(flag.NoOptDefVal) != 0 {
739				// We had assumed dealing with a two-word flag but the flag is a boolean flag.
740				// In that case, there is no value following it, so we are not really doing flag completion.
741				// Reset everything to do noun completion.
742				trimmedArgs = args
743				flag = nil
744			}
745		}
746
747		return flag, trimmedArgs, lastArg, nil
748	}
749
750	// InitDefaultCompletionCmd adds a default 'completion' command to c.
751	// This function will do nothing if any of the following is true:
752	// 1- the feature has been explicitly disabled by the program,
753	// 2- c has no subcommands (to avoid creating one),
754	// 3- c already has a 'completion' command provided by the program.
755	func (c *Command) InitDefaultCompletionCmd(args ...string) {
756		if c.CompletionOptions.DisableDefaultCmd {
757			return
758		}
759
760		for _, cmd := range c.commands {
761			if cmd.Name() == compCmdName || cmd.HasAlias(compCmdName) {
762				// A completion command is already available
763				return
764			}
765		}
766
767		haveNoDescFlag := !c.CompletionOptions.DisableNoDescFlag && !c.CompletionOptions.DisableDescriptions
768
769		// Special case to know if there are sub-commands or not.
770		hasSubCommands := false
771		for _, cmd := range c.commands {
772			if cmd.Name() != ShellCompRequestCmd && cmd.Name() != helpCommandName {
773				// We found a real sub-command (not 'help' or '__complete')
774				hasSubCommands = true
775				break
776			}
777		}
778
779		completionCmd := &Command{
780			Use:   compCmdName,
781			Short: "Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell",
782			Long: fmt.Sprintf(`Generate the autocompletion script for %[1]s for the specified shell.
783	See each sub-command's help for details on how to use the generated script.
784	`, c.Root().Name()),
785			Args:              NoArgs,
786			ValidArgsFunction: NoFileCompletions,
787			Hidden:            c.CompletionOptions.HiddenDefaultCmd,
788			GroupID:           c.completionCommandGroupID,
789		}
790		c.AddCommand(completionCmd)
791
792		if !hasSubCommands {
793			// If the 'completion' command will be the only sub-command,
794			// we only create it if it is actually being called.
795			// This avoids breaking programs that would suddenly find themselves with
796			// a subcommand, which would prevent them from accepting arguments.
797			// We also create the 'completion' command if the user is triggering
798			// shell completion for it (prog __complete completion '')
799			subCmd, cmdArgs, err := c.Find(args)
800			if err != nil || subCmd.Name() != compCmdName &&
801				(subCmd.Name() != ShellCompRequestCmd || len(cmdArgs) <= 1 || cmdArgs[0] != compCmdName) {
802				// The completion command is not being called or being completed so we remove it.
803				c.RemoveCommand(completionCmd)
804				return
805			}
806		}
807
808		out := c.OutOrStdout()
809		noDesc := c.CompletionOptions.DisableDescriptions
810		shortDesc := "Generate the autocompletion script for %s"
811		bash := &Command{
812			Use:   "bash",
813			Short: fmt.Sprintf(shortDesc, "bash"),
814			Long: fmt.Sprintf(`Generate the autocompletion script for the bash shell.
815
816	This script depends on the 'bash-completion' package.
817	If it is not installed already, you can install it via your OS's package manager.
818
819	To load completions in your current shell session:
820
821		source <(%[1]s completion bash)
822
823	To load completions for every new session, execute once:
824
825	#### Linux:
826
827		%[1]s completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/%[1]s
828
829	#### macOS:
830
831		%[1]s completion bash > $(brew --prefix)/etc/bash_completion.d/%[1]s
832
833	You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.
834	`, c.Root().Name()),
835			Args:                  NoArgs,
836			DisableFlagsInUseLine: true,
837			ValidArgsFunction:     NoFileCompletions,
838			RunE: func(cmd *Command, args []string) error {
839				return cmd.Root().GenBashCompletionV2(out, !noDesc)
840			},
841		}
842		if haveNoDescFlag {
843			bash.Flags().BoolVar(&noDesc, compCmdNoDescFlagName, compCmdNoDescFlagDefault, compCmdNoDescFlagDesc)
844		}
845
846		zsh := &Command{
847			Use:   "zsh",
848			Short: fmt.Sprintf(shortDesc, "zsh"),
849			Long: fmt.Sprintf(`Generate the autocompletion script for the zsh shell.
850
851	If shell completion is not already enabled in your environment you will need
852	to enable it.  You can execute the following once:
853
854		echo "autoload -U compinit; compinit" >> ~/.zshrc
855
856	To load completions in your current shell session:
857
858		source <(%[1]s completion zsh)
859
860	To load completions for every new session, execute once:
861
862	#### Linux:
863
864		%[1]s completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_%[1]s"
865
866	#### macOS:
867
868		%[1]s completion zsh > $(brew --prefix)/share/zsh/site-functions/_%[1]s
869
870	You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.
871	`, c.Root().Name()),
872			Args:              NoArgs,
873			ValidArgsFunction: NoFileCompletions,
874			RunE: func(cmd *Command, args []string) error {
875				if noDesc {
876					return cmd.Root().GenZshCompletionNoDesc(out)
877				}
878				return cmd.Root().GenZshCompletion(out)
879			},
880		}
881		if haveNoDescFlag {
882			zsh.Flags().BoolVar(&noDesc, compCmdNoDescFlagName, compCmdNoDescFlagDefault, compCmdNoDescFlagDesc)
883		}
884
885		fish := &Command{
886			Use:   "fish",
887			Short: fmt.Sprintf(shortDesc, "fish"),
888			Long: fmt.Sprintf(`Generate the autocompletion script for the fish shell.
889
890	To load completions in your current shell session:
891
892		%[1]s completion fish | source
893
894	To load completions for every new session, execute once:
895
896		%[1]s completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/%[1]s.fish
897
898	You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.
899	`, c.Root().Name()),
900			Args:              NoArgs,
901			ValidArgsFunction: NoFileCompletions,
902			RunE: func(cmd *Command, args []string) error {
903				return cmd.Root().GenFishCompletion(out, !noDesc)
904			},
905		}
906		if haveNoDescFlag {
907			fish.Flags().BoolVar(&noDesc, compCmdNoDescFlagName, compCmdNoDescFlagDefault, compCmdNoDescFlagDesc)
908		}
909
910		powershell := &Command{
911			Use:   "powershell",
912			Short: fmt.Sprintf(shortDesc, "powershell"),
913			Long: fmt.Sprintf(`Generate the autocompletion script for powershell.
914
915	To load completions in your current shell session:
916
917		%[1]s completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression
918
919	To load completions for every new session, add the output of the above command
920	to your powershell profile.
921	`, c.Root().Name()),
922			Args:              NoArgs,
923			ValidArgsFunction: NoFileCompletions,
924			RunE: func(cmd *Command, args []string) error {
925				if noDesc {
926					return cmd.Root().GenPowerShellCompletion(out)
927				}
928				return cmd.Root().GenPowerShellCompletionWithDesc(out)
929
930			},
931		}
932		if haveNoDescFlag {
933			powershell.Flags().BoolVar(&noDesc, compCmdNoDescFlagName, compCmdNoDescFlagDefault, compCmdNoDescFlagDesc)
934		}
935
936		completionCmd.AddCommand(bash, zsh, fish, powershell)
937	}

939	func findFlag(cmd *Command, name string) *pflag.Flag {
940		flagSet := cmd.Flags()
941		if len(name) == 1 {
942			// First convert the short flag into a long flag
943			// as the cmd.Flag() search only accepts long flags
944			if short := flagSet.ShorthandLookup(name); short != nil {
945				name = short.Name
946			} else {
947				set := cmd.InheritedFlags()
948				if short = set.ShorthandLookup(name); short != nil {
949					name = short.Name
950				} else {
951					return nil
952				}
953			}
954		}
955		return cmd.Flag(name)
956	}

958	// CompDebug prints the specified string to the same file as where the
959	// completion script prints its logs.
960	// Note that completion printouts should never be on stdout as they would
961	// be wrongly interpreted as actual completion choices by the completion script.
962	func CompDebug(msg string, printToStdErr bool) {
963		msg = fmt.Sprintf("[Debug] %s", msg)
964
965		// Such logs are only printed when the user has set the environment
966		// variable BASH_COMP_DEBUG_FILE to the path of some file to be used.
967		if path := os.Getenv("BASH_COMP_DEBUG_FILE"); path != "" {
968			//nolint:gosec // G703:BASH_COMP_DEBUG_FILE intentionally user-controlled for completion debug logging.
969			f, err := os.OpenFile(path,
970				os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
971			if err == nil {
972				defer f.Close()
973				WriteStringAndCheck(f, msg)
974			}
975		}
976
977		if printToStdErr {
978			// Must print to stderr for this not to be read by the completion script.
979			fmt.Fprint(os.Stderr, msg)
980		}
981	}
982
983	// CompDebugln prints the specified string with a newline at the end
984	// to the same file as where the completion script prints its logs.
985	// Such logs are only printed when the user has set the environment
986	// variable BASH_COMP_DEBUG_FILE to the path of some file to be used.
987	func CompDebugln(msg string, printToStdErr bool) {
988		CompDebug(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n", msg), printToStdErr)
989	}
990
991	// CompError prints the specified completion message to stderr.
992	func CompError(msg string) {
993		msg = fmt.Sprintf("[Error] %s", msg)
994		CompDebug(msg, true)
995	}
996
997	// CompErrorln prints the specified completion message to stderr with a newline at the end.
998	func CompErrorln(msg string) {
999		CompError(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n", msg))
1000	}

1002	// These values should not be changed: users will be using them explicitly.
1003	const (
1004		configEnvVarGlobalPrefix       = "COBRA"
1005		configEnvVarSuffixDescriptions = "COMPLETION_DESCRIPTIONS"
1006	)
1007
1008	var configEnvVarPrefixSubstRegexp = regexp.MustCompile(`[^A-Z0-9_]`)
1009
1010	// configEnvVar returns the name of the program-specific configuration environment
1011	// variable.  It has the format <PROGRAM>_<SUFFIX> where <PROGRAM> is the name of the
1012	// root command in upper case, with all non-ASCII-alphanumeric characters replaced by `_`.
1013	func configEnvVar(name, suffix string) string {
1014		// This format should not be changed: users will be using it explicitly.
1015		v := strings.ToUpper(fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s", name, suffix))
1016		v = configEnvVarPrefixSubstRegexp.ReplaceAllString(v, "_")
1017		return v
1018	}
1019
1020	// getEnvConfig returns the value of the configuration environment variable
1021	// <PROGRAM>_<SUFFIX> where <PROGRAM> is the name of the root command in upper
1022	// case, with all non-ASCII-alphanumeric characters replaced by `_`.
1023	// If the value is empty or not set, the value of the environment variable
1024	// COBRA_<SUFFIX> is returned instead.
1025	func getEnvConfig(cmd *Command, suffix string) string {
1026		v := os.Getenv(configEnvVar(cmd.Root().Name(), suffix))
1027		if v == "" {
1028			v = os.Getenv(configEnvVar(configEnvVarGlobalPrefix, suffix))
1029		}
1030		return v
1031	}

**Handoff**

**Invariant:** `completionInput` must return `trimmedArgs` whose backing array is distinct from the caller-provided `args` slice. The comment at lines 317–320 documents exactly this: downstream code appends sentinel values (e.g. `"--"` on line 379) to the argument list, and those appends must not overwrite the original `args` elements—especially the `toComplete` word.

**Current owner:** `completionInput` itself, lines 321–325 in `completions.go`. The `append([]string(nil), args[:len(args)-1]...)` idiom is the explicit enforcement point.

**Change that would silently violate it:** Simplifying the body to a bare slice expression like `trimmedArgs = args[:len(args)-1]`. That would make `trimmedArgs` share the same backing array. The sentinel append at line 379 (`append(finalArgs, "--")`) would then spill into the `args` slots, silently overwriting the last element. While today's caller (`getCompletions`) doesn't read `args` afterward, any future reader of `args` after the call—or any caller that reuses the `args` slice—would see a corrupted value. The invariant exists because the completion machinery is designed to keep the caller's input immutable.
